name: Scaloppine Al Lemon
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Veal scallops, cut 3/8-inch thick and pounded until 1/4-inch thick
3/4 cup Beef stock, fresh or canned
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal scallops with freshly ground black pepper.
2. Dip the veal scallops in flour and shake off excess.
3. In a heavy skillet, melt margarine and olive oil over moderate heat.
4. When foam subsides, add 4-5 veal scallops at a time and sauté until golden brown.
5. Transfer the veal scallops to a plate. Pour off most of the fat from the skillet, leaving a thin film on the bottom.
6. Add 3/4 cup beef stock and let it boil briskly for a few minutes, stirring constantly.
7. Scrape in any browned bits clinging to the bottom and sides of the pan.
8. Return the veal scallops to the skillet and arrange lemon slices on top.
9. Cover the skillet and simmer over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the veal is tender when pierced with a knife.
10. Transfer the scallops to a heated platter and surround with lemon slices.
11. Add the remaining beef stock to the juices in the skillet and oil. Briskly cook until the stock turns into a syrupy glaze.
12. Add the lemon juice and cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
13. Pour the sauce over the scallops. Serves 6.
Note: Veal cubes (stewing meat) may be substituted. Pound as flat as possible. Follow directions above, but cooking time will be increased to 45 minutes to an hour over low heat or until tender.
